reasoning: We are at November 9, 2025, with less than two months remaining in the timeframe (until December 31, 2025). Despite intensive US airstrikes against Houthi targets in the spring and throughout 2025, analysts and officials have consistently characterized the deployment of regular US ground troops to Yemen as "highly unlikely." Ground campaigns against the Houthis are expected to rely on local proxy forces rather than US regulars. Furthermore, the resolution criteria strictly exclude special operations forces, military advisors, and diplomatic entourages—the only types of US personnel with a historical or current terrestrial presence in Yemen. With no credible reporting of regular US troops landing in Yemen between March and November 2025, and no announced plans to deploy any before the end of the year, the probability of this occurring is exceedingly low.
